text.title: BABOK Six Knowledge Areas
text.summary: Purpose, key tasks, outputs, and linked methodologies for all six BABOK Knowledge Areas.

section.title: KA-1: Business Analysis Planning and Monitoring
section.p: Purpose: plan how BA work will be done and track performance.

list.item: Plan BA Approach — define how BA will be performed; output: BA approach document.
Plan Stakeholder Engagement — identify and plan work with stakeholders; output: stakeholder engagement approach.
Plan Governance — define decision-making for requirements; output: governance approach.
Plan Information Management — define how BA info is stored and accessed; output: information management approach.
Identify Improvements — assess BA performance, recommend improvements.

section.title: KA-2: Elicitation and Collaboration
section.p: Purpose: draw out information from stakeholders and confirm results.

list.item: Prepare for Elicitation — understand scope, select techniques; output: elicitation activity plan.
Conduct Elicitation — draw out relevant information; output: elicitation results (unconfirmed).
Confirm Results — verify understanding with stakeholders; output: elicitation results (confirmed).
Communicate BA Information — ensure shared understanding.
Manage Collaboration — encourage stakeholders to work together.

section.p: Linked methodologies: elicitation-techniques. Techniques: interviews, workshops, observation, surveys, document analysis, prototyping, brainstorming, focus groups.

section.title: KA-3: Requirements Lifecycle Management
section.p: Purpose: manage requirements through their full lifecycle.

list.item: Trace Requirements — ensure alignment at different levels; output: requirements (traced).
Maintain Requirements — keep accurate and consistent; output: requirements (maintained).
Prioritize Requirements — rank by importance; output: requirements (prioritized).
Assess Changes — evaluate proposed changes; output: change assessment.
Approve Requirements — obtain agreement; output: requirements (approved).

section.p: Linked methodologies: requirements-traceability, requirements-lifecycle, requirements-prioritization. Outputs: traceability matrix, requirements baseline, change impact analysis.

section.title: KA-4: Strategy Analysis
section.p: Purpose: understand the current state and define the change strategy to achieve the future state.

list.item: Analyze Current State — understand existing environment; output: current state description.
Define Future State — define goals and objectives; output: future state description.
Assess Risks — identify uncertainties; output: risk assessment results.
Define Change Strategy — develop approach to achieve future state; output: change strategy.

section.p: Linked methodologies: strategy-analysis. Techniques: gap analysis, SWOT, business model canvas, capability assessment, risk analysis.

section.title: KA-5: Requirements Analysis and Design Definition
section.p: Purpose: structure, specify, model, verify, and validate requirements; define design options.

list.item: Specify and Model Requirements — output: requirements (specified/modeled).
Verify Requirements — ensure quality standards met; output: requirements (verified).
Validate Requirements — ensure alignment with business goals; output: requirements (validated).
Define Requirements Architecture — ensure requirements support each other.
Define Design Options — define solution approaches.
Analyze Value and Recommend — assess options, recommend solution.

section.p: Linked methodologies: use-case-modeling, user-story-mapping, data-analysis, business-process-analysis, decision-analysis, interface-analysis, requirements-validation, acceptance-criteria, requirements-documentation.

section.title: KA-6: Solution Evaluation
section.p: Purpose: assess solution performance and the value it delivers.

list.item: Measure Solution Performance — define how performance is measured; output: performance measures.
Analyze Performance Measures — examine data against expected value; output: performance analysis.
Assess Solution Limitations — investigate problems and causes; output: solution limitations.
Assess Enterprise Limitations — investigate enterprise factor impacts.
Recommend Actions — identify actions to increase value; output: value improvement recommendations.

section.p: Linked methodologies: solution-assessment. Outputs: performance metrics, value realization report, improvement recommendations, lessons learned.
